Ill- 039/4012009 A] 3--rew 4471-171 -(cta-7. MINISTRY OF HOME AFFAIRS NOTIFICAlION New Delhi, the 15th February, 2010 G.S. R. 80(E).- Whereas the Central Government is of the opinion that for the purposes specified in sub-section (1) of section 153 of Sashastra Scema Bal Act, 2007 (53 of2007),- (a) an officer of the rank corresponding to that of the lowest rank of member of the "Sashastra 576 0112010 (I) -- 1 of 2 -- 2 _ THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY Seema Bal" is empowered under the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1973 (2 of 1974) to exercise and discharge the powers and duties under sections 41 (I), 46, 47, 51(1), 52,102,149,150, 151 and l52 of the said Code; an officer of the rank corresponding to superior officer and subordinate officer of the Force is empowered under the said Code to exercise and discharge the powers and duties under sections 41(2), 48, 53, 100, 129 and 131 thereof; Now, therefore, in exercise of the powers conferred by sub-section (1) of section 153 of Sashastra Seema B al Act, 2007 (53 of 2007). the Central Government hereby empowers the Sashastra Seema Hal as follows. namely • (a) that any member of the Sashastra Seema Bal may, within the local limits of the areas sped- lied in the Schedule hereto annexed exercise SPAR 11—SEC. 3(i)] and discharge the powers and duties under sections 41(1), 46, 47, 51( I). 52, 102, 149, 150, 151 and 152 of the Criminal Procedure Code, 1973 (2 of 1974); and any superior officer or subordinate officers of the Sashastra Seema Hal may, within the local limits of the area specified in the said Schedule, exercise and discharge the powers and duties under section 41(2), 48, 53. 100, 129 and 131 of the said Code. SCHEDULE So much of the area comprised within a belt of 15 kilometers in the States of Uttarakhand. Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, West Bengal, Sikkim, Assam and Arunachal Pradesh running along the Indo-Nepal and Indo-Bhutan Border, and in any area of its operation. [F. No. 111-110391402009-G1 ASHOK LAVASA, Jt. Secy.
